title: Shared Memory & Synchronization Barriers
description: Covers Local Data Share (LDS/groupshared) as a programmer-managed scratchpad that allows threads in the same group to share intermediate results, along with the GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync / barrier synchronization primitives required for safe access. LDS is the enabling technology for efficient separable blurs, image reduction operations, and the prefix-sum algorithms used in GPU-driven indirect buffer generation. Students will implement a separable Gaussian blur entirely in a single compute pass using LDS ping-pong, achieving bandwidth reduction versus a two-pass separable approach, and profile the LDS utilization in NSight.
